name: Browserbase Cloud Browser API
what_it_does: Managed cloud browser service providing scalable, headless Playwright and CDP browser sessions designed specifically for AI agents doing web automation.
use_cases: AI agent web automation requiring a real browser (JavaScript rendering, auth flows, captcha solving)
Running Playwright or Puppeteer scripts in the cloud without managing browser infrastructure
LLM-driven web browsing using the Stagehand library built on top of Browserbase
Parallel browser sessions for multi-step agent workflows
Recording and replaying agent browser sessions for debugging and auditing
not_for: Simple HTTP data fetching where a browser is not required (use a direct HTTP client instead)
High-volume scraping of static HTML pages (more expensive than lightweight scrapers)
Applications with strict data residency requirements outside of US/EU
best_when: An agent must interact with a real browser — handling JavaScript, cookies, authentication, or complex UI flows — and you want managed infrastructure rather than self-hosting browsers.
avoid_when: The target site returns static HTML or has an official API; browser overhead adds cost and latency that is not justified.

auth.notes: API key is used to create browser sessions via the REST API and passed via the Browserbase Playwright driver. Keys are account-scoped with no granular permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 100 browser minutes per month on the free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: From approximately $0.015 per browser minute; volume plans available for higher usage
pricing.notes: Billing is by active browser session time. Idle sessions still accrue cost; agents should close sessions promptly. Long-running sessions can get expensive quickly.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent session limits depend on plan; free tier allows limited parallelism

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Session errors surface through the Playwright CDP connection with clear codes. REST API errors are well-structured JSON. The Stagehand SDK provides higher-level error abstractions for LLM-driven steps.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Session creation is not idempotent; each API call creates a new billable session. Browser actions within a session are stateful. Agents should track session IDs to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Forgetting to close sessions after task completion results in unexpected billing charges
Session timeout defaults may terminate long-running agent tasks; configure maxSessionLength appropriately
Stagehand LLM-driven actions consume additional AI API tokens on top of browser time costs
Some anti-bot measures (Cloudflare, reCAPTCHA) may still block sessions despite the managed environment
Cold start for a new browser session adds 1-3 seconds before the page can be loaded

security_score_components.security_notes: Each browser session runs in an isolated container. Credentials passed to browser sessions (for logging into sites) are ephemeral. No SOC2 certification publicly documented. API keys have no scope restrictions.
